pages.full_text:
Gloucester–Hereford canal, lots of Navvies buried in the churchyard.
Ashperton tunnel unstable.
    We left early to get back to Sunday church at St. G.  Simon lost van key.

__Wednesday July 6th__
9.15. Simon, Finder & Eve drove to Charles' & Jenny's new house in Oxford, Frenchay Rd off Woodstock Rd.  A week ago he beat the nasty woman next door in planning permission for a window.  House full of workmen working on shell.  Rooms clear.  V. exciting.                                          Haypr
On to deliver lights Watlington & [?send] Hinley.
On to Peter & Nana's house at Finsbury Pk.
Our last visit before they move after July 19th.
We slept in van & had breakfast with Nana & Thomas.  Peter in Wales & SW with Conrad, Eleni & [?Satter]-in-law Chhatrapati.  Nana was doing a dance exhibition which we had to miss.  After watching a bit of Murray at Wimbledon we went to the Merchant Taylors, gorgeous, & had a BBQ in courtyard put on by [illegible].  We (Chad) had invited 3 designers.
pages.notes: Top of page continues from a previous spread (mid-sentence beginning 'Gloucester–Hereford canal'). The first few lines appear to be in pink/magenta ink, then text switches to black. 'Ashperton' is a village in Herefordshire on the Hereford & Gloucester Canal route. 'Haypr' in the right margin is unclear — possibly a surname or note. 'Finder' is an unusual name — could be a nickname or misread; possibly 'Tinder' or 'Finder'. 'Hinley' may be a place or a person's name (could read 'Henley'). 'Finsbury Pk' = Finsbury Park, London. 'Merchant Taylors' = Merchant Taylors' Hall, London. '[?Satter]-in-law Chhatrapati' — 'Satter' uncertain, 'Chhatrapati' is an Indian name used as a surname here. Year not given on page; 'Murray at Wimbledon' and 'July 6th Wednesday' would narrow to specific years (e.g. 2011, 2016) but cannot be confirmed without external verification.

pages.full_text:
We tried to speak to as many designers as possible.  I sat next to a very nice Irishman who did sea diving & had 3 daughters & a (small) salvage ~~card~~ company.  He did the joinery on the QE2.

__Thursday__
    It's been so hot today & yesterday we've had to keep the chocolate in the fridge.
We went to the end of year London Show.
Jason (the designer's husband) gave us tickets.  It was WOW!
A man on [?Bham] stand had a stool made of felt – actually plastic bottle felt.  Compacted.  Simon liked the texture & wants to make lights out of it.
Another man wants to cut down on carbon footprint of light-making.  He puts maple chips
                                                              ← mushroom
    maple                                                         mould
                                                                 spores.
in a mould &
Ingots in a mould                     + heat 200
like dry rot.                              for 20 min.
pages.notes: Top of page continues from left page mid-entry. 'salvage ~~card~~ company' — the word 'card' appears struck through/crossed out. '[?Bham] stand' — likely 'Birmingham stand' abbreviated. 'QE2' refers to the ocean liner Queen Elizabeth 2. The right page includes informal diagram notes about a manufacturing process (maple chips, mushroom mould spores, heat 200 for 20 min) arranged around hand-drawn annotations — these are not a route sketch but process notes embedded in the text. 'Jason (the designer's husband)' — 'Jason' and 'the designer' (presumably Sue or similar) are personal names, not places. 'London Show' — likely referring to a design trade show in London. Year cannot be determined from the page content alone.
